body.title: [sprint] Lingering uncommitted changes / stashes raise a system_alarm (CP6 clean-tree, made literal)
body.intention: I intend that abandoned uncommitted work — a dirty working tree or a lingering git stash — surfaces as a system alarm instead of rotting silently, because that is exactly the should-not-happen, weeks-of-invisibility condition the alarm primitive exists to catch (CP6 clean-tree / no-stash, made literal).
body.description:
**Problem:** 3 CC stashes sat invisible for 3 weeks (work behind 'done' tickets that never shipped). CP6 says clean-tree / no-stash, but nothing enforces or surfaces a violation — so abandoned WIP rots until a git accident exposes it. This is precisely the should-not-happen-and-stays-invisible pattern system_alarms was built for.

**Shape:** A detection function that, for a given repo, checks for (a) a dirty working tree (uncommitted tracked changes) and (b) lingering stashes, and calls raise_alarm with a stable signature per condition — e.g. `dirty-tree:<repo>` and `lingering-stash:<repo>` — caller = the checking context, message = a human one-liner (file count / stash count + age). The alarm then surfaces on the ALARMS PANEL and tmux nag like any other. Self-clears when the tree goes clean (the alarm ages out via prune_stale / can be closed). NEVER auto-commits, auto-cleans, or drops a stash — surfacing only; humans decide (no escape hatch, CP6).

**Affected files:** a new small checker (e.g. unseen_university/git_hygiene_alarm.py) calling unseen_university.system_alarms.raise_alarm; no changes to the primitive.
**Design rules:** fail-soft (a broken check never breaks the caller); read-only git (status/stash list); flat-file alarm store (already resilient); never destroy WIP.
**Scope boundary:** IN — the detection + raise_alarm wiring with stable signatures, returning what it alarmed. OUT — WHERE/when it runs (which device sweeps it — separate wiring ticket); any auto-remediation; the salvage of the current 3 stashes (separate review).
**Test plan:** a temp git repo with an uncommitted change raises `dirty-tree:<repo>` once (deduped on re-check); a repo with a stash raises `lingering-stash:<repo>`; a clean repo with no stashes raises nothing; the checker never raises an exception even if git is absent.
**Completion criteria:** the checker drops `dirty-tree:<repo>` when the tree has uncommitted changes and `lingering-stash:<repo>` when stashes exist, deduped by signature; a clean repo produces no alarm; the checker is fail-soft (returns, never throws) and never mutates git state.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
